Phytolacca octandra is a plant species that belongs to the family Phytolaccaceae, commonly known as the Pokeweed family. 

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help to treat conditions such as arthritis and wounds.

The bioactive constituents of Phytolacca octandra include saponins, flavonoids, and anthraquinones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Phytolacca octandra is typically used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions.
